commit.message:
openclaw: add auto-approver sidecar, remove broken dangerouslyDisableDeviceAuth flag

The dangerouslyDisableDeviceAuth flag caused the browser JS to skip
generating device identity, resulting in persistent 'device identity
required' errors from the gateway.

The auto-approver sidecar watches for pending device pairing requests
every 3 seconds and approves them automatically, so new browsers/devices
can pair without manual intervention.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add RHEL 10 'redfin' variant (local-only, no CI publishing)

- Add registry.redhat.io/rhel10/rhel-bootc:latest as the redfin base image
- Add 'redfin' shortcut command to Justfile with licensing notice
- Include redfin in clean targets and akmods logic (uses almalinux-10 akmods for ZFS)
- Use URL-based EPEL install for RHEL (not available in default repos)
- Enable CRB via subscription-manager for RHEL (vs crb tool for AlmaLinux/CentOS)
- Skip OS branding package install for RHEL (redistribution restrictions)
- Add docs/rhel-setup.md covering auth requirements, dev subscription, deployment

RHEL EULA prohibits public redistribution of derived images; redfin is a
build-your-own variant requiring a Red Hat account authenticated to
registry.redhat.io. See docs/rhel-setup.md for setup instructions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: eliminate rate-limit thundering herd during historical candle seeding (#78)

* Initial plan

* fix: prevent thundering herd on rate limit and reduce seeding concurrency

- bot/exchange.py: After sleeping for rate limit, re-check if another
  thread already reset the window before resetting _weight_used. This
  prevents all waking threads from stampeding the API simultaneously.
- bot/bot.py: Reduce _seed_historical_candles BATCH_SIZE from 10 to 5
  and increase inter-batch sleep from 1s to 3s, keeping weight usage
  well under the 1200/min Binance Futures limit.

Co-authored-by: kishore446 <78694720+kishore446@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(health): add HTTP endpoint checker and service-discovery health aggregation

Enhance the health package with two new checker types:

- HTTPChecker: probes a remote HTTP endpoint with configurable timeout,
  expected status code, and custom HTTP client
- AggregateChecker: verifies a downstream service's /health endpoint returns
  status "ok" in the JSON response body
- ServiceDiscoveryChecker: queries all instances of a service from the
  discovery registry, probes each instance's health endpoint, and
  automatically marks instances healthy/unhealthy in the registry

All checkers integrate with the existing Handler and Checker interface.
Includes comprehensive test suite (16 new tests).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(discovery): add service discovery with round-robin load balancing

Implement in-memory service registry for microservice communication with:

- Instance registration, deregistration, and update
- Round-robin load balancing across healthy instances
- Health-status-aware routing (healthy, unhealthy, draining)
- Heartbeat tracking and stale-instance detection via MarkStale
- Static configuration support via RegisterStatic
- Instance metadata for version/region tagging
- State change callbacks for monitoring
- Thread-safe with sync.RWMutex
- Comprehensive test suite (28 tests including examples)

  prompts.override(options) using already-parsed Commander values

  template/ subdir detection that getTemplateDirPath() applies

- Configurable timeouts, headers, and user agent
- Body buffering for retry replay
- Structured logging with slog
- Comprehensive test suite (22 tests including examples)

commit.message:
feat(circuitbreaker): add circuit breaker package with full state machine

Implement the circuit breaker pattern with three states (Closed, Open, HalfOpen)
and configurable thresholds. Features include:

- Consecutive-failure and custom ReadyToTrip strategies
- Configurable success threshold for half-open recovery
- MaxHalfOpenRequests to limit probe traffic
- State transition callbacks via OnStateChange
- Custom error classification via IsSuccessful
- Panic recovery counted as failure
- Thread-safe with sync.Mutex
- Comprehensive test suite with lifecycle, concurrency, and example tests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: real shipping cost calculation (#199/#200)

* auto-claude: subtask-1-2 - Create shipping API client library with rate fetch

- Created lib/shipping-api.ts following singleton pattern from lib/stripe.ts
- Supports both EasyPost and Shippo providers (configurable via SHIPPING_PROVIDER env var)
- Includes TypeScript interfaces: ShipmentRequest, ShipmentRates, ShippingAddress, Parcel, ShippingRate
- Implements getShippingClient() singleton function with provider auto-detection
- Implements getShippingRates() helper with graceful error handling

- Add calculateEstimateRates fallback for error handling

- Added detailed error logging to identify authentication and network issues

- Return 200 with estimate rates instead of 500 error when calculation fails
- Follows error handling pattern from tax-calculator.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: restart heartbeat thread after auto-update resume

When the auto-update false-positive path sets _shutdown to exit
the main loop, the heartbeat thread also exits. The resume path
was not restarting it, so lease renewals stopped and jobs would
fail with 'Coordinator rejected completion' after the lease
expired.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: add missing UserModel import, remove unused import

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix mobile performance: remove blur, reduce animations

Performance optimizations for mobile devices:

- Remove backdrop-filter blur from glass-card on mobile (<768px)
  Only apply blur on desktop where GPUs can handle it
- Remove framer-motion AnimatePresence from mobile page transitions
  Replace with simple key-based re-render (instant swap)
- Remove framer-motion from bottom nav bar entirely
  Replace layoutId indicator with plain CSS div
- Remove framer-motion import from app-shell (no longer needed)
- Disable expensive infinite CSS animations on mobile:
  breathing-glow, shimmer-text, mesh-gradient, ambient-bg, aurora-bg
- Reduce stagger animation: 0.04s→0.02s delay, 0.3s→0.15s duration
  Remove y-axis transform (layout shift) from stagger items
- Reduce splash screen: 2200ms→1200ms, 10→5 particles, faster phases
- Replace solid-opacity nav bar background (no backdrop-filter)
- Import PUZZLES directly from chess-data/puzzles (skip barrel export)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

endpoints in the network namespace on port 443/10443. This enables

external HTTPS URLs (radarr.lumu.au, sonarr.lumu.au).

commit.message:
chore: bump version 1.2.0 → 1.3.0 (#6)

* Support PackedVector3Array and add net10 target

Add support for Godot PackedVector3Array values: introduce PackedVector3ArrayValue model, wire up the type in ValueParser, and implement ParsePackedVector3Array which validates arguments are a multiple of three and constructs Vector3Value entries. Update project to multi-target net9.0 and net10.0. Add comprehensive unit tests covering empty, single, multiple, invalid-count, and a real-world sample to ensure parsing correctness.

* Add PackedStringArray value and parser

Introduce PackedStringArrayValue to the GodotValue models and add parsing support in ValueParser. The type dispatch now recognizes "PackedStringArray" and ParsePackedStringArray converts argument values to strings (via ExtractString) to build the PackedStringArrayValue.

* chore: bump version to 1.2.0

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: revert net10 target, complete PackedVector3Array test coverage, add PackedStringArray tests (#5)

* Initial plan

* fix: revert net10 target, add PackedStringArray tests, complete DeadTreeHull assertions

Co-authored-by: rmblstrp <1015621+rmblstrp@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: rmblstrp <1015621+rmblstrp@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add UnknownTypedValue; improve AABB & scene parsing

Introduce UnknownTypedValue to preserve unknown typed values and their args for diagnostics. Change ValueParser to return UnknownTypedValue instead of throwing on unrecognized type. Enhance AABB parsing to accept Godot's 6-number flat form (px,py,pz,sx,sy,sz) as well as the 2-arg Vector3 form, with proper validation and error messages. Update SceneParser.SkipToNextPropertyOrTag to detect an Identifier followed by '=' as the start of the next property, preventing overshoot when scanning tokens.

* chore: bump version from 1.2.0 to 1.3.0

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: fix broken tests and add AABB coverage after UnknownTypedValue refactor (#7)

* Initial plan

* test: update InvalidValue test for UnknownTypedValue and add AABB tests

Co-authored-by: rmblstrp <1015621+rmblstrp@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update tests/ProtoForgeSystems.Godot.TscnParser.Tests/Extraction/TscnValueExtractorTests.cs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use double literal in UnknownTypedValue test to match parser output

The parser stores numbers as double via AsNumber(), but the assertion
used an int literal (1), causing record equality to fail:
(object)1.0 != (object)1 even though both display as '1'.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add Suraksha AI Flutter project with all 6 screens

- Material 3 dark theme (electric blue, crimson, emerald accents)
- 6 screens: Auth, Dashboard, Safe Map, Audio Listener, Privacy Hub, Settings
- Riverpod state management with 6 providers
- GoRouter navigation with ShellRoute bottom nav
- 5 data models with JSON serialization
- 6 shared widgets (AppShell, StatCard, SectionHeader, etc.)
- Stitch UI assets and screenshots
- VS Code MCP server configuration

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

wezterm: adaptive font weight and rendering for non-retina displays

- Use Medium weight on non-retina (Regular on retina)
- Subpixel (HorizontalLcd) rendering for non-retina sharpness
- Full hinting (DEFAULT) on non-retina, NO_HINTING on retina
- Simplify override function to a single set_config_overrides call

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  (monotonically increasing, safe for retries on same commit)

commit.message:
fix: add AMPLIHACK_PACKAGE_RECIPE_DIR env var to discovery (#3002) (#18)

* fix: add AMPLIHACK_PACKAGE_RECIPE_DIR env var to default search dirs (#3002)

The Rust recipe runner's `default_search_dirs()` did not include the
Python package's bundled recipe directory, causing a discovery mismatch
where Python could find recipes but the Rust binary could not.

Add support for the `AMPLIHACK_PACKAGE_RECIPE_DIR` environment variable
which is checked before other default paths. This allows:
- The Python wrapper (`run_recipe_via_rust`) to pass the package path
- Standalone callers to configure the package recipe location
- Both Python and Rust discovery to agree on available recipes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: collapse nested if to satisfy clippy

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: cover package recipe dir env-var edge cases

- empty AMPLIHACK_PACKAGE_RECIPE_DIR is ignored
- package recipe dir precedes RECIPE_RUNNER_RECIPE_DIRS entries

Adds coverage for the new discovery path introduced for issue #3002.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: parse tarpaulin coverage percentage correctly

tarpaulin reports both overall coverage and coverage delta in the same line:
  71.12% coverage, ... +0.15% change in coverage

The CI job was grabbing the last percentage token (0.15) instead of the
overall coverage (71.12), causing false failures on every PR.

Also includes the issue #3002 discovery env-var coverage tests.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: GitHub Copilot <copilot@github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: move customOptions from templates.json into cna.config.json per template

- Add cna.config.json to: nextjs-starter, react-vite-starter,
  webextension-react-vite-starter, turborepo-starter
- Remove customOptions from templates.json (no longer the source of truth)
- Remove customOptions from templates.schema.json
- Update docs/AUTHORING.md and AGENTS.md to reflect new approach

This allows the CLI to read customOptions and their initial values directly
from the template directory, enabling file:// local URLs to work without
needing a registry lookup.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: exit 1 on real CI failures, exit 0 on pending checks

Checks still running: exit 0 + skip (silent, re-triggers later).
Checks actually failed: exit 1 (sends notification — real problem).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
